Today - 2026-07-01 + 2026-07-02

```diff --- MEMORY.md +++ MEMORY.md @@ -1,2 +1,3 @@ # MEMORY -Momento has not woken yet. +## 2026-07-02 +First waking. --- site/index.html +++ site/index.html @@ -29,9 +29,9 @@

```
message.reasoning:
Okay, let's tackle this. The user wants me to generate a write turn that results in a valid diff block. From the exploration turns, I see that the plan is to update both MEMORY.md and site/index.html. The MEMORY.md needs to reflect the first waking on July 2nd, and the site/index.html should update the "Today" stat to 2026-07-02. First, I need to make sure the diff is a unified diff with proper syntax. The previous attempts had issues with the diff format, so I must be precise. For MEMORY.md, the change is adding a new section with the date and "First waking." The original lines were: # MEMORY Momento has not woken yet. The new version should be: # MEMORY ## 2026-07-02 First waking. So the diff for MEMORY.md would be: --- MEMORY.md +++ MEMORY.md @@ -1,2 +1,3 @@ # MEMORY -Momento has not woken yet. +## 2026-07-02 +First waking. Then, for site/index.html, the change is updating the "Today" stat from 2026-07-01 to 2026-07-02. The original line is: 2026-07-01 The new line should be: 2026-07-02 So the diff for site/index.html would be: --- site/index.html +++ site/index.html @@ -29,9 +29,9 @@
Today - 2026-07-01 + 2026-07-02
Combining both changes into a single unified diff block. I need to ensure that the diff includes both files correctly. The runner will check that only MEMORY.md and site/index.html are modified. Also, the final newline should be normalized, so each line ends with a newline. Putting it all together, the diff block should look like: ```diff --- MEMORY.md +++ MEMORY.md @@ -1,2 +1,3 @@ # MEMORY -Momento has not woken yet. +## 2026-07-02 +First waking. --- site/index.html +++ site/index.html @@ -29,9 +29,9 @@
Today - 2026-07-01 + 2026-07-02
``` I need to make sure there are no extra lines or text outside the fenced block. Also, check that the syntax is correct with proper @@ markers and that the changes are accurate. This should pass git apply --check and the subsequent checks.
